# Part 10 — App Driving {.sdaia-dark background-gradient="linear-gradient(135deg, #1C355E, #00C9A7)"}

## The Dabble Gamepad

We will use the **Gamepad Module** in the Dabble app to drive our robot. The Gamepad has buttons like Up, Down, Left, and Right.

![Dabble Gamepad Module](https://ai.thestempedia.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/Game-Pad.jpg)

## Reading the Gamepad

In our `loop()`, we need to continuously check if the Dabble app is sending any button presses:

```cpp
void loop() {
  Dabble.processInput(); // Refresh data from the smartphone
  
  if (GamePad.isUpPressed()) {
    moveForward();
  } 
  else if (GamePad.isDownPressed()) {
    moveBackward(); // Assuming we have this function
  }
  else if (GamePad.isLeftPressed()) {
    moveLeft(); // Assuming we have this function
  }
  else if (GamePad.isRightPressed()) {
    moveRight(); // Assuming we have this function
  }
  else {
    stopCar();
  }
}
```

## Creating the Helper Functions

To keep our code clean, we use custom functions like `moveForward()`.

```cpp
void moveForward() {
  // Left Motor Forward
  digitalWrite(IN1, HIGH);
  digitalWrite(IN2, LOW);
  
  // Right Motor Forward
  digitalWrite(IN3, HIGH);
  digitalWrite(IN4, LOW);
}

void stopCar() {
  digitalWrite(IN1, LOW);
  digitalWrite(IN2, LOW);
  digitalWrite(IN3, LOW);
  digitalWrite(IN4, LOW);
}
```

## The Driving Challenge

Now it's time to test! 

1. Upload the code to your Arduino Uno.
2. Disconnect the USB cable (very important!).
3. Turn on the battery pack.
4. Pair your phone via Bluetooth to your HM-10 (Password is usually `1234` or `000000`).
5. Open the Dabble app, select Gamepad, and drive!
